104.22: When the sun rises, they steal away and lie down in their dens.
104.23: Man goes out to his work and to his labor until the evening.
104.24: O LORD, how manifold are your works! In wisdom you have made them all; the earth is full of your creatures.
104.25: Here is the sea, great and wide, which teems with creatures without number, living things both small and great.
104.26: There go the ships, and Leviathan, which you formed to play in it.
104.27: These all look to you, to give them their food in due season.
104.28: When you give it to them, they gather it up; when you open your hand, they are filled with good things.
104.29: When you hide your face, they are dismayed; when you take away their breath, they die and return to their dust.
104.30: When you send forth your spirit, they are created, and you renew the face of the ground.
104.31: May the glory of the LORD endure forever; may the LORD rejoice in his works!
104.32: He looks at the earth and it trembles; he touches the mountains and they smoke.
104.33: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will make music to my God while I have breath.
104.34: May my meditation be pleasing to him; I will rejoice in the LORD.
104.35: Let sinners be consumed from the earth, and let the wicked be no more! Bless the LORD, O my soul! Praise the LORD!

97.1: The LORD reigns; let the earth rejoice! Let the many coastlands be glad!
97.2: Clouds and thick darkness are round about him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.
97.3: Fire goes before him and burns up his enemies on every side.
97.4: His lightnings lit up the world; the earth saw and trembled.
97.5: The mountains melt like wax before the LORD, before the Lord of all the earth.
97.6: The heavens declare his righteousness, and all peoples see his glory.
97.7: All who worship carved images are put to shame — those who boast in idols. All gods bow down before him.
97.8: Zion heard and was glad, and the daughters of Judah rejoiced because of your judgments, O LORD.
97.9: For you, O LORD, are most high over all the earth; you are exalted far above all gods.
97.10: O you who love the LORD, hate evil! He preserves the souls of his faithful; he delivers them from the hand of the wicked.
97.11: Light is sown for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart.
97.12: Rejoice in the LORD, O you righteous, and give thanks to his holy name!

98.1: A Psalm. O sing to the LORD a new song, for he has done marvelous things! His right hand and his holy arm have worked salvation for him.
98.2: The LORD has made known his salvation; he has revealed his righteousness in the sight of the nations.
98.3: He has remembered his steadfast love and his faithfulness to the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
98.4: Make a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth! Break forth into joyful song and sing praises!
98.5: Sing praises to the LORD with the harp, with the harp and the voice of melody!
98.6: With trumpets and the sound of the horn make a joyful noise before the King, the LORD!
98.7: Let the sea roar, and all that fills it; the world and those who dwell in it!
98.8: Let the rivers clap their hands; let the hills sing together for joy!
98.9: Before the LORD, for he comes to judge the earth! He will judge the world with righteousness and the peoples with equity.

99.1: The LORD reigns; let the peoples tremble! He sits enthroned between the cherubim; let the earth quake!
99.2: The LORD is great in Zion; he is exalted above all the peoples.
99.3: Let them praise your great and awesome name — holy is he!
99.4: The king in his strength loves justice. You have established equity; you have executed justice and righteousness in Jacob.
99.5: Exalt the LORD our God; worship at his footstool — holy is he!
99.6: Moses and Aaron were among his priests; Samuel also was among those who called on his name. They called to the LORD and he answered them.
99.7: In the pillar of cloud he spoke to them; they kept his testimonies and the decree he gave them.
99.8: O LORD our God, you answered them; you were a forgiving God to them, but an avenger of their wrongdoings.
99.9: Exalt the LORD our God and worship at his holy mountain, for the LORD our God is holy!

100.1: A Psalm for giving thanks. Make a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth!
100.2: Serve the LORD with gladness! Come before his presence with singing!
100.3: Know that the LORD is God! It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people and the sheep of his pasture.
100.4: Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise! Give thanks to him; bless his name!
100.5: For the LORD is good; his steadfast love endures forever, and his faithfulness to all generations.

101.1: A Psalm of David. I will sing of steadfast love and justice; to you, O LORD, I will make music.
101.2: I will behave wisely in a blameless way. O when will you come to me? I will walk with integrity of heart within my house.
101.3: I will not set before my eyes any worthless thing. I hate the doing of those who turn aside; it shall not cling to me.
101.4: A perverse heart shall be far from me; I will not know evil.
101.5: Whoever slanders his neighbor in secret I will destroy. A haughty look and an arrogant heart I will not endure.
101.6: My eyes shall be on the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me; whoever walks in the blameless way shall minister to me.
101.7: No one who practices deceit shall dwell in my house; no one who utters lies shall stand before my eyes.
101.8: Morning by morning I will destroy all the wicked of the land, cutting off all evildoers from the city of the LORD.

102.1: A Prayer of an afflicted man, when he is overwhelmed and pours out his complaint before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD; let my cry come to you!
102.2: Do not hide your face from me in the day of my distress! Incline your ear to me; answer me speedily in the day when I call!
102.3: For my days pass away like smoke, and my bones burn like a furnace.
102.4: My heart is struck down like grass and has withered; I forget to eat my bread.
102.5: Because of my loud groaning my bones cling to my flesh.
102.6: I am like a desert owl, like an owl of the waste places.
102.7: I lie awake; I am like a lonely sparrow on the housetop.
102.8: All the day my enemies taunt me; those who mock me use my name as a curse.
102.9: For I eat ashes like bread and mingle tears with my drink,
102.10: because of your indignation and your anger; for you have taken me up and thrown me down.
102.11: My days are like a lengthening shadow; I wither away like grass.
102.12: But you, O LORD, are enthroned forever; your name endures to all generations.
102.13: You will arise and have pity on Zion; it is the time to show her favor — the appointed time has come.
102.14: For your servants cherish her stones and show pity for her dust.
102.15: The nations will fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth your glory.
102.16: For the LORD will build up Zion; he will appear in his glory.
102.17: He regards the prayer of the destitute and does not despise their prayer.
102.18: Let this be recorded for a generation to come, so that a people yet to be created may praise the LORD.
102.19: That he looked down from his holy height; from heaven the LORD looked at the earth,
102.20: to hear the groaning of the prisoner, to set free those who were doomed to die,
102.21: that they may declare in Zion the name of the LORD and his praise in Jerusalem,
102.22: when peoples are gathered together and kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
102.23: He has broken my strength in the middle of my way; he has cut short my days.
102.24: I said, 'O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days — you whose years endure through all generations!'
102.25: Of old you laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
102.26: They will perish, but you will remain; they will all wear out like a garment. You will change them like a robe, and they will pass away,
102.27: but you are the same, and your years will not come to an end.
102.28: The children of your servants shall dwell secure; their offspring shall be established before you.

105.1: O give thanks to the LORD; call upon his name; make known his deeds among the peoples!
105.2: Sing to him, sing praises to him; meditate on all his wondrous works!
105.3: Glory in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice!
105.4: Seek the LORD and his strength; seek his face continually!
105.5: Remember the wondrous works he has done, his marvels and the ordinances of his mouth,
105.6: O offspring of Abraham his servant, O sons of Jacob his chosen ones!
105.7: He is the LORD our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
105.8: He has remembered his covenant forever, the word he commanded to a thousand generations —
105.9: the covenant which he made with Abraham, and his sworn oath to Isaac,
105.10: which he confirmed to Jacob as a statute, to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
105.11: saying, 'To you I will give the land of Canaan as the lot of your inheritance.'
105.12: When they were but few in number, very few, and sojourners in it,
105.13: wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people,
105.14: he permitted no man to oppress them, and he rebuked kings on their account:
105.15: 'Do not touch my anointed ones; do my prophets no harm!'
105.16: He called a famine upon the land; he broke every staff of bread.
105.17: He sent a man before them — Joseph, who was sold as a servant.
105.18: His feet they afflicted with fetters; iron entered into his soul.
105.19: Until the time when his word came to pass, the word of the LORD tested him.
105.20: The king sent and released him; the ruler of peoples let him go free.
105.21: He made him lord of his house and ruler over all his possessions,
105.22: to bind his princes at his pleasure, and to teach his elders wisdom.
105.23: Then Israel also came into Egypt, and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
105.24: And he made his people very fruitful and made them stronger than their adversaries.
105.25: He turned their hearts to hate his people, to deal craftily with his servants.
105.26: He sent Moses his servant, and Aaron whom he had chosen.
105.27: They performed his signs among them and worked wonders in the land of Ham.
105.28: He sent darkness and made it dark; they did not rebel against his word.
105.29: He turned their waters to blood and caused their fish to die.
105.30: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the chambers of their kings.
105.31: He spoke and there came swarms of flies and gnats throughout all their territory.
105.32: He gave them hail for rain, and flaming fire throughout their land.
105.33: He struck their vines and their fig trees and shattered the trees throughout their territory.
105.34: He spoke and the locusts came, and young locusts without number,
105.35: and devoured all the vegetation in their land and ate up the fruit of their ground.
105.36: He struck all the firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their strength.
105.37: Then he brought them out with silver and gold, and none among his tribes was feeble.
105.38: Egypt was glad when they departed, for the fear of them had fallen upon it.
105.39: He spread a cloud for a covering, and fire to give light in the night.
105.40: They asked, and he brought quail, and satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
105.41: He opened the rock and waters gushed out; they ran through the desert like a river.
105.42: For he remembered his holy word to Abraham his servant.
105.43: So he brought out his people with joy, and his chosen ones with singing.
105.44: And he gave them the lands of the nations, and they took possession of the labor of the peoples,
105.45: that they might keep his statutes and observe his laws. Praise the LORD!

107.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever!
107.2: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so — those he has redeemed from the hand of the enemy,
107.3: and gathered from the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the sea.
107.4: Some wandered in a desert wilderness, finding no path to a city to dwell in.
107.5: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
107.6: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distresses.
107.7: He led them by a straight way until they reached a city to dwell in.
107.8: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to the children of man!
107.9: For he satisfies the longing soul, and the hungry soul he fills with good things.
107.10: Some sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, prisoners in affliction and iron —
107.11: for they had rebelled against the words of God and despised the counsel of the Most High.
107.12: So he humbled their heart with hard labor; they stumbled, with none to help.
107.13: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them from their distresses.
107.14: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death and shattered their bonds.
107.15: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to the children of man!
107.16: For he has shattered the doors of bronze and cut the bars of iron in two.
107.17: Some were fools; they were afflicted because of their sinful way and because of their iniquities.
107.18: Their soul loathed every kind of food, and they drew near to the gates of death.
107.19: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them from their distresses.
107.20: He sent out his word and healed them and delivered them from their destructions.
107.21: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to the children of man!
107.22: And let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving and declare his works with singing.
107.23: Those who went down to the sea in ships and did their business on the great waters —
107.24: these saw the works of the LORD, his wonders in the deep.
107.25: For he spoke and raised up a stormy wind, which lifted up its waves.
107.26: They mounted up to the heavens and went down to the depths; their soul melted away in their calamity.
107.27: They reeled and staggered like a drunkard, and all their skill was swallowed up.
107.28: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he brought them out of their distresses.
107.29: He stilled the storm to a calm, so that its waves were hushed.
107.30: Then they were glad because they were quiet, and he led them to their desired haven.
107.31: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to the children of man!
107.32: Let them exalt him in the congregation of the people and praise him in the assembly of the elders.
107.33: He turns rivers into a desert and springs of water into thirsty ground,
107.34: a fruitful land into a salt waste, for the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
107.35: He turns the desert into pools of water and dry land into springs of water.
107.36: And there he settles the hungry to dwell, so that they establish a city to live in.
107.37: They sow fields and plant vineyards that yield a fruitful harvest.
107.38: He blesses them so they multiply greatly, and he does not let their livestock decrease.
107.39: When they are diminished and brought low through oppression, trouble, and sorrow,
107.40: he pours contempt on princes and makes them wander in trackless wastes;
107.41: but he lifts the needy out of their affliction and makes their families like flocks.
107.42: The upright see it and are glad, and all wickedness shuts its mouth.
107.43: Whoever is wise, let him observe these things and consider the steadfast love of the LORD.

108.1: A Song. A Psalm of David. My heart is steadfast, O God; I will sing and make music, even with my glory.
108.2: Awake, harp and lyre! I myself will awaken the dawn.
108.3: I will give thanks to you, O LORD, among the peoples and I will sing praises to you among the nations.
108.4: For your steadfast love is great above the heavens, and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
108.5: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens! Let your glory be over all the earth!
108.6: That your beloved may be delivered — save us by your right hand and answer us.
108.7: God has spoken in his holiness: I will exult; I will divide Shechem and portion out the valley of Succoth.
108.8: Gilead is mine; Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is the helmet of my head; Judah is my scepter.
108.9: Moab is my washbasin; over Edom I cast my sandal; over Philistia I shout in triumph.
108.10: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
108.11: Have you not rejected us, O God? And you, O God, do you not go out with our armies?
108.12: Give us help against our foes, for the help of man is worthless.
108.13: With God we shall act valiantly, for it is he who will tread down our enemies.

106.1: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever!
106.2: Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD, or declare all his praise?
106.3: Blessed are those who keep justice, who do righteousness at all times!
106.4: Remember me, O LORD, with the favor you show your people; visit me with your salvation,
106.5: that I may look upon the prosperity of your chosen ones, that I may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, that I may glory with your inheritance.
106.6: We have sinned, along with our fathers; we have committed iniquity; we have acted wickedly.
106.7: Our fathers, when they were in Egypt, did not consider your wondrous works; they did not remember the multitude of your steadfast love; but they rebelled at the sea, at the Red Sea.
106.8: Yet he saved them for the sake of his name, to make known his mighty power.
106.9: He rebuked the Red Sea, and it dried up; he led them through the deep as through a desert.
106.10: So he saved them from the hand of him who hated them and redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
106.11: The waters covered their adversaries; not one of them was left.
106.12: Then they believed his words; they sang his praise.
106.13: But they soon forgot his works; they did not wait for his counsel.
106.14: They craved intensely in the wilderness and tested God in the desert.
106.15: He gave them what they asked, but sent leanness into their bodies.
106.16: When men in the camp were jealous of Moses and of Aaron, the holy one of the LORD,
106.17: the earth opened and swallowed up Dathan and covered the company of Abiram.
106.18: Fire also broke out against their company; a flame burned up the wicked.
106.19: They made a calf in Horeb and worshipped a cast image.
106.20: They exchanged their glory for the likeness of an ox that eats grass.
106.21: They forgot God their Savior, who had done great things in Egypt,
106.22: wondrous works in the land of Ham and awesome deeds by the Red Sea.
106.23: Therefore he said he would destroy them — had not Moses, his chosen one, stood in the breach before him to turn his wrath from destroying them.
106.24: They despised the pleasant land; they did not believe his word.
106.25: They murmured in their tents and did not listen to the voice of the LORD.
106.26: Therefore he raised his hand and swore to them that he would make them fall in the wilderness,
106.27: and would scatter their offspring among the nations, dispersing them through the lands.
106.28: Then they yoked themselves to Baal of Peor and ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
106.29: They provoked the LORD to anger with their deeds, and a plague broke out among them.
106.30: Then Phinehas stood up and intervened, and the plague was stayed.
106.31: And that was counted to him as righteousness, from generation to generation forever.
106.32: They angered him at the waters of Meribah, and it went ill with Moses on their account,
106.33: for they made his spirit bitter, so that he spoke rashly with his lips.
106.34: They did not destroy the peoples, as the LORD commanded them,
106.35: but they mingled with the nations and learned to do as they did.
106.36: They served their idols, which became a snare to them.
106.37: They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons,
106.38: and shed innocent blood, the blood of their sons and daughters, sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; and the land was polluted with blood.
106.39: Thus they became unclean by their deeds and went whoring in their works.
106.40: Then the anger of the LORD burned hot against his people, and he abhorred his inheritance.
106.41: He gave them into the hand of the nations, so that those who hated them ruled over them.
106.42: Their enemies oppressed them, and they were brought low under their hand.
106.43: Many times he delivered them, but they were rebellious in their counsel and were brought low by their iniquity.
106.44: Nevertheless he regarded their distress when he heard their cry.
106.45: For their sake he remembered his covenant and relented according to the abundance of his steadfast love.
106.46: He caused them to be pitied by all who held them captive.
106.47: Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the nations, to give thanks to your holy name and to glory in your praise.
106.48: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting! And let all the people say, 'Amen!' Praise the LORD!

115.1: Not to us, O LORD, not to us, but to your name give glory, for the sake of your steadfast love and your faithfulness.
115.2: Why should the nations say, 'Where now is their God?'
115.3: But our God is in the heavens; he does whatever he pleases.
115.4: Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
115.5: Mouths have they, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not.
115.6: Ears have they, but they hear not; noses have they, but they smell not.
115.7: They have hands, but they handle not; feet have they, but they walk not; they make no sound in their throat.
115.8: Those who make them will become like them, as will all who trust in them.
115.9: O Israel, trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
115.10: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
115.11: You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
115.12: The LORD has been mindful of us; he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron;
115.13: he will bless those who fear the LORD, both the small and the great.
115.14: May the LORD give you increase, you and your children!
115.15: May you be blessed by the LORD, who made heaven and earth!
115.16: The heavens are the LORD's heavens, but the earth he has given to the children of man.
115.17: The dead do not praise the LORD, nor do any who go down into silence.
115.18: But we will bless the LORD from this time forth and forevermore. Praise the LORD!

116.1: I love the LORD, for he has heard my voice and my supplications.
116.2: Because he inclined his ear to me, I will call on him as long as I live.
116.3: The cords of death encompassed me; the terrors of Sheol laid hold of me; I found distress and anguish.
116.4: Then I called on the name of the LORD: 'O LORD, I beseech you, deliver my soul!'
116.5: Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; yes, our God is merciful.
116.6: The LORD preserves the simple; when I was brought low, he saved me.
116.7: Return, O my soul, to your rest, for the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.
116.8: For you have delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116.9: I will walk before the LORD in the land of the living.
116.10: I believed, even when I spoke: 'I am greatly afflicted.'
116.11: I said in my alarm, 'All men are liars.'
116.12: What shall I return to the LORD for all his benefits to me?
116.13: I will lift up the cup of salvation and call on the name of the LORD.
116.14: I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people.
116.15: Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his saints.
116.16: O LORD, I am your servant; I am your servant, the son of your maidservant. You have loosed my bonds.
116.17: I will offer to you the sacrifice of thanksgiving and call on the name of the LORD.
116.18: I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people,
116.19: in the courts of the house of the LORD, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

117.1: Praise the LORD, all you nations! Extol him, all you peoples!
117.2: For great is his steadfast love toward us, and the faithfulness of the LORD endures forever. Praise the LORD!

118.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever.
118.2: Let Israel now say: 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118.3: Let the house of Aaron now say: 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118.4: Let those who fear the LORD now say: 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118.5: Out of my distress I called on the LORD; the LORD answered me and set me in a wide place.
118.6: The LORD is for me; I will not fear. What can man do to me?
118.7: The LORD is for me among my helpers; I will look in satisfaction on those who hate me.
118.8: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in man.
118.9: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in princes.
118.10: All nations surrounded me; in the name of the LORD I cut them off.
118.11: They surrounded me, yes, surrounded me on every side; in the name of the LORD I cut them off.
118.12: They surrounded me like bees; they were extinguished like a fire of thorns; in the name of the LORD I cut them off.
118.13: I was pushed hard so that I was falling, but the LORD helped me.
118.14: The LORD is my strength and my song; he has become my salvation.
118.15: The sound of joyful song is in the tents of the righteous: 'The right hand of the LORD acts valiantly;'
118.16: the right hand of the LORD is exalted, the right hand of the LORD acts valiantly.
118.17: I shall not die but live, and I will declare the works of the LORD.
118.18: The LORD has chastened me severely, but he has not given me over to death.
118.19: Open to me the gates of righteousness; I will enter through them and give thanks to the LORD.
118.20: This is the gate of the LORD; the righteous shall enter through it.
118.21: I give thanks to you, for you have answered me and have become my salvation.
118.22: The stone that the builders rejected has become the head of the corner.
118.23: This is the LORD's doing; it is marvelous in our eyes.
118.24: This is the day that the LORD has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
118.25: O LORD, save us, we pray! O LORD, grant us success, we pray!
118.26: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD! We bless you from the house of the LORD.
118.27: The LORD is God, and he has made his light to shine upon us. Bind the festal sacrifice with cords, even to the horns of the altar.
118.28: You are my God, and I will give thanks to you; you are my God, I will exalt you.
118.29: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever.

109.1: A Psalm of David. O God of my praise, do not hold your peace!
109.2: For the mouths of the wicked and the deceitful are opened against me; they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
109.3: They have surrounded me with words of hatred and attacked me without cause.
109.4: In return for my love they accuse me, but I am in prayer.
109.5: They have repaid me evil for good and hatred for my love.
109.6: Appoint a wicked man over him; let an accuser stand at his right hand.
109.7: When he is judged, let him be found guilty; let his prayer be counted as sin.
109.8: Let his days be few; let another take his office.
109.9: Let his children become fatherless and his wife a widow.
109.10: Let his children wander and beg and seek their bread from their desolate places.
109.11: Let the creditor seize all that he has; let strangers plunder the fruit of his labor.
109.12: Let there be none to extend steadfast love to him, nor any to show favor to his fatherless children.
109.13: Let his posterity be cut off; let their name be blotted out in the following generation.
109.14: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, and let not his mother's sin be blotted out.
109.15: Let them be before the LORD continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.
109.16: Because he did not remember to show steadfast love, but pursued the poor and needy and the brokenhearted, to put them to death.
109.17: He loved cursing; let it come upon him! He did not delight in blessing; let it be far from him!
109.18: He clothed himself with cursing as his garment; let it enter his body like water and his bones like oil.
109.19: Let it be like a garment that he wraps around him, like a belt that he wears every day.
109.20: This is the reward of my adversaries from the LORD — of those who speak evil against my soul.
109.21: But you, O Lord GOD, act on my behalf for your name's sake; because your steadfast love is good, deliver me!
109.22: For I am poor and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.
109.23: I fade like a lengthening shadow; I am shaken off like a locust.
109.24: My knees are weak from fasting, and my flesh has become thin, without fat.
109.25: I have become a reproach to them; when they see me, they shake their heads.
109.26: Help me, O LORD my God! Save me according to your steadfast love.
109.27: Let them know that this is your hand — that you, O LORD, have done it.
109.28: Let them curse, but you will bless; when they arise, let them be put to shame, but may your servant rejoice.
109.29: Let my accusers be clothed with dishonor; let them be wrapped in their own shame as in a cloak.
109.30: I will give great thanks to the LORD with my mouth; I will praise him in the midst of the multitude.
109.31: For he stands at the right hand of the needy, to save him from those who condemn his soul.

110.1: A Psalm of David. The declaration of the LORD to my Lord: Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies your footstool.
110.2: The LORD sends forth from Zion your mighty scepter. Rule in the midst of your enemies!
110.3: Your people offer themselves willingly on the day of your power, in holy splendor; from the womb of the morning, the dew of your youth is yours.
110.4: The LORD has sworn and will not relent: You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.
110.5: The Lord is at your right hand; he will shatter kings on the day of his wrath.
110.6: He will execute judgment among the nations, filling them with corpses; he will shatter the chief over the wide earth.
110.7: He will drink from the brook by the way; therefore he will lift up his head.

111.1: Praise the LORD! I will give thanks to the LORD with my whole heart, in the company of the upright, in the congregation.
111.2: Great are the works of the LORD, sought out by all who delight in them.
111.3: His work is full of honor and majesty, and his righteousness endures forever.
111.4: He has made his wonderful works to be remembered; the LORD is gracious and compassionate.
111.5: He has given food to those who fear him; he remembers his covenant forever.
111.6: He has shown his people the power of his works, in giving them the inheritance of the nations.
111.7: The works of his hands are faithfulness and justice; all his precepts are sure.
111.8: They stand firm forever and ever, performed with faithfulness and uprightness.
111.9: He sent redemption to his people; he has ordained his covenant forever. Holy and awesome is his name.
111.10: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; a good understanding have all those who practice it. His praise endures forever.

112.1: Praise the LORD! Blessed is the man who fears the LORD, who greatly delights in his commandments.
112.2: His offspring will be mighty in the land; the generation of the upright will be blessed.
112.3: Wealth and riches are in his house, and his righteousness endures forever.
112.4: Light dawns in the darkness for the upright; he is gracious, compassionate, and righteous.
112.5: Good is the man who is generous and lends; who conducts his affairs with justice.
112.6: For the righteous will never be moved; he will be remembered forever.
112.7: He is not afraid of evil tidings; his heart is firm, trusting in the LORD.
112.8: His heart is established; he will not be afraid, until he looks in triumph on his adversaries.
112.9: He has distributed freely; he has given to the poor; his righteousness endures forever; his horn is exalted in honor.
112.10: The wicked man sees it and is angry; he gnashes his teeth and melts away; the desire of the wicked will perish.

113.1: Praise the LORD! Praise, O servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD!
113.2: Blessed be the name of the LORD from this time forth and forevermore!
113.3: From the rising of the sun to its setting, the name of the LORD is to be praised!
113.4: The LORD is high above all nations, and his glory is above the heavens.
113.5: Who is like the LORD our God, who dwells on high,
113.6: who humbles himself to look upon the heavens and the earth?
113.7: He raises the poor from the dust and lifts the needy from the ash heap,
113.8: to make them sit with princes, with the princes of his people.
113.9: He gives the barren woman a home, making her a joyful mother of children. Praise the LORD!

114.1: When Israel went out from Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
114.2: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his dominion.
114.3: The sea saw it and fled; the Jordan turned back.
114.4: The mountains skipped like rams and the hills like young lambs.
114.5: What ailed you, O sea, that you fled? O Jordan, that you turned back?
114.6: O mountains, that you skipped like rams? O hills, like young lambs?
114.7: Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob,
114.8: who turns the rock into a pool of water, the flint into a spring of water.

120.1: A Song of Ascents. In my distress I cried to the LORD, and he answered me.
120.2: O LORD, deliver my soul from lying lips, from a deceitful tongue.
120.3: What shall be given to you, and what shall be added to you, O deceitful tongue?
120.4: Sharp arrows of a warrior, with glowing coals of the broom tree!
120.5: Woe to me, that I sojourn in Meshech, that I dwell among the tents of Kedar!
120.6: Too long has my soul had its dwelling among those who hate peace.
120.7: I am for peace, but when I speak, they are for war.

121.1: A Song of Ascents. I lift up my eyes to the hills. From where does my help come?
121.2: My help comes from the LORD, who made heaven and earth.
121.3: He will not let your foot stumble; he who keeps you will not slumber.
121.4: Behold, he who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.
121.5: The LORD is your keeper; the LORD is your shade on your right hand.
121.6: The sun shall not strike you by day, nor the moon by night.
121.7: The LORD will keep you from all evil; he will keep your soul.
121.8: The LORD will keep your going out and your coming in from this time forth and forevermore.

122.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. I was glad when they said to me, 'Let us go to the house of the LORD!'
122.2: Our feet are standing within your gates, O Jerusalem!
122.3: Jerusalem — built as a city that is bound firmly together,
122.4: to which the tribes go up, the tribes of the LORD, as was decreed for Israel, to give thanks to the name of the LORD.
122.5: There thrones of judgment were set, the thrones of the house of David.
122.6: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem! May those who love you prosper.
122.7: May peace be within your walls, and security within your towers.
122.8: For the sake of my brothers and companions I will say, 'Peace be within you!'
122.9: For the sake of the house of the LORD our God I will seek your good.

123.1: A Song of Ascents. To you I lift up my eyes, O you who are enthroned in the heavens!
123.2: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the LORD our God, until he has mercy on us.
123.3: Have mercy on us, O LORD, have mercy on us, for we have had more than enough of contempt.
123.4: Our soul has had more than enough of the scoffing of those who are at ease, of the contempt of the proud.

124.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. If it had not been the LORD who was on our side — let Israel now say —
124.2: if it had not been the LORD who was on our side when people rose up against us,
124.3: then they would have swallowed us up alive, when their anger was kindled against us;
124.4: then the flood would have swept us away, the torrent would have gone over our soul;
124.5: then the raging waters would have gone over our soul.
124.6: Blessed be the LORD, who has not given us as prey to their teeth!
124.7: We have escaped like a bird from the snare of the fowlers; the snare is broken, and we have escaped!
124.8: Our help is in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth.

125.1: A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved but abides forever.
125.2: As the mountains are round about Jerusalem, so the LORD is round about his people, from this time forth and forevermore.
125.3: For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest on the land allotted to the righteous, lest the righteous stretch out their hands to do wrong.
125.4: Do good, O LORD, to those who are good, and to those who are upright in their hearts.
125.5: But those who turn aside to their crooked ways the LORD will lead away with the workers of iniquity. Peace be upon Israel!

119.1: ALEPH. Blessed are those whose way is blameless, who walk in the law of the LORD!
119.2: Blessed are those who keep his testimonies and seek him with their whole heart,
119.3: who also do no iniquity but walk in his ways.
119.4: You have commanded your precepts to be kept diligently.
119.5: O that my ways were steadfast to keep your statutes!
119.6: Then I would not be put to shame when I look to all your commandments.
119.7: I will praise you with an upright heart when I have learned your righteous judgments.
119.8: I will keep your statutes — O do not forsake me utterly!
119.9: BETH. How can a young man keep his way clean? By guarding it according to your word.
119.10: With my whole heart I have sought you; let me not stray from your commandments.
119.11: Your word I have stored up in my heart so that I might not sin against you.
119.12: Blessed are you, O LORD; teach me your statutes!
119.13: With my lips I have recounted all the judgments of your mouth.
119.14: In the way of your testimonies I rejoice as much as in all riches.
119.15: I will meditate on your precepts and look to your ways.
119.16: I will delight in your statutes; I will not forget your word.
119.17: GIMEL. Deal bountifully with your servant, that I may live and keep your word.
119.18: Open my eyes, that I may behold wondrous things out of your law.
119.19: I am a stranger on the earth; hide not your commandments from me.
119.20: My soul is consumed with longing for your judgments at all times.
119.21: You have rebuked the proud, those who are cursed, who stray from your commandments.
119.22: Remove from me reproach and contempt, for I have kept your testimonies.
119.23: Even though princes sit and speak against me, your servant meditates on your statutes.
119.24: Your testimonies are my delight and my counselors.
119.25: DALETH. My soul clings to the dust; revive me according to your word.
119.26: I have declared my ways and you answered me; teach me your statutes.
119.27: Make me understand the way of your precepts, and I will meditate on your wondrous works.
119.28: My soul melts for sorrow; strengthen me according to your word.
119.29: Remove from me the way of lying and graciously grant me your law.
119.30: I have chosen the way of truth; your judgments I have laid before me.
119.31: I have held fast to your testimonies, O LORD; let me not be put to shame.
119.32: I run in the way of your commandments when you enlarge my heart.
119.33: HE. Teach me, O LORD, the way of your statutes, and I will keep it to the end.
119.34: Give me understanding that I may keep your law and observe it with my whole heart.
119.35: Lead me in the path of your commandments, for I delight in it.
119.36: Incline my heart to your testimonies and not toward dishonest gain.
119.37: Turn away my eyes from looking at vanity and revive me in your ways.
119.38: Confirm your word to your servant, you who are devoted to your fear.
119.39: Turn away the reproach which I dread, for your judgments are good.
119.40: Behold, I long for your precepts; revive me in your righteousness.
119.41: VAU. Let your steadfast love come to me, O LORD, your salvation according to your word;
119.42: that I may have an answer for him who taunts me, for I trust in your word.
119.43: And do not take the word of truth utterly out of my mouth, for I have hoped in your judgments.
119.44: So shall I keep your law continually, forever and ever.
119.45: And I will walk in a wide place, for I have sought your precepts.
119.46: I will also speak of your testimonies before kings and will not be ashamed.
119.47: I will delight myself in your commandments, which I love.
119.48: I will lift up my hands toward your commandments, which I love, and I will meditate on your statutes.
119.49: ZAIN. Remember your word to your servant, on which you have made me hope.
119.50: This is my comfort in my affliction: that your word has given me life.
119.51: The arrogant have utterly mocked me, yet I have not turned from your law.
119.52: I remember your judgments of old, O LORD, and I am comforted.
119.53: Horror has seized me because of the wicked who forsake your law.
119.54: Your statutes have been my songs in the house of my pilgrimage.
119.55: I have remembered your name in the night, O LORD, and have kept your law.
119.56: This blessing I have had because I kept your precepts.
119.57: CHETH. You are my portion, O LORD; I promise to keep your words.
119.58: I sought your favor with my whole heart; be gracious to me according to your word.
119.59: When I considered my ways, I turned my feet to your testimonies.
119.60: I hurry and do not delay to keep your commandments.
119.61: The cords of the wicked have entangled me, but I have not forgotten your law.
119.62: At midnight I rise to give you thanks for your righteous judgments.
119.63: I am a companion of all who fear you and of those who keep your precepts.
119.64: The earth is full of your steadfast love, O LORD; teach me your statutes.
119.65: TETH. You have dealt well with your servant, O LORD, according to your word.
119.66: Teach me good judgment and knowledge, for I believe in your commandments.
119.67: Before I was afflicted I strayed, but now I keep your word.
119.68: You are good and do good; teach me your statutes.
119.69: The proud have smeared lies against me, but I will keep your precepts with my whole heart.
119.70: Their heart is gross like grease, but I delight in your law.
119.71: It is good for me that I was afflicted, that I might learn your statutes.
119.72: The law of your mouth is better to me than thousands of pieces of gold and silver.
119.73: JOD. Your hands have made me and fashioned me; give me understanding to learn your commandments.
119.74: Those who fear you will see me and be glad, because I have hoped in your word.
119.75: I know, O LORD, that your judgments are right and that in faithfulness you have afflicted me.
119.76: Let your steadfast love be my comfort according to your promise to your servant.
119.77: Let your tender mercies come to me that I may live, for your law is my delight.
119.78: Let the proud be put to shame, for they have wronged me without cause; I will meditate on your precepts.
119.79: Let those who fear you turn to me, those who know your testimonies.
119.80: Let my heart be blameless in your statutes so that I may not be ashamed.
119.81: CAPH. My soul faints for your salvation, but I hope in your word.
119.82: My eyes fail with watching for your word; I ask, 'When will you comfort me?'
119.83: For I have become like a wineskin in the smoke, yet I have not forgotten your statutes.
119.84: How long must your servant endure? When will you judge those who pursue me?
119.85: The arrogant have dug pits for me; they do not live by your law.
119.86: All your commandments are faithful; they persecute me wrongfully — help me!
119.87: They have all but done away with me on earth, but I have not forsaken your precepts.
119.88: Revive me according to your steadfast love, that I may keep the testimonies of your mouth.
119.89: LAMED. Forever, O LORD, your word is firmly established in the heavens.
119.90: Your faithfulness endures through all generations; you established the earth and it stands fast.
119.91: They continue to this day according to your ordinances, for all things are your servants.
119.92: If your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction.
119.93: I will never forget your precepts, for by them you have given me life.
119.94: I am yours; save me, for I have sought your precepts.
119.95: The wicked lie in wait to destroy me, but I consider your testimonies.
119.96: I have seen a limit to all perfection, but your commandment is exceedingly broad.
119.97: MEM. O how I love your law! It is my meditation all the day.
119.98: Your commandment makes me wiser than my enemies, for it is ever with me.
119.99: I have more insight than all my teachers, for your testimonies are my meditation.
119.100: I understand more than the elders, because I keep your precepts.
119.101: I have held back my feet from every evil way in order to keep your word.
119.102: I have not departed from your judgments, for you have taught me.
119.103: How sweet are your words to my taste, sweeter than honey to my mouth!
119.104: Through your precepts I get understanding; therefore I hate every false way.
119.105: NUN. Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.
119.106: I have sworn and confirmed it: I will keep your righteous judgments.
119.107: I am severely afflicted; revive me, O LORD, according to your word!
119.108: Accept, O LORD, the freewill offerings of my mouth, and teach me your judgments.
119.109: My life is in my hand continually, yet I do not forget your law.
119.110: The wicked have set a snare for me, but I have not strayed from your precepts.
119.111: Your testimonies are my heritage forever, for they are the joy of my heart.
119.112: I have inclined my heart to perform your statutes forever, to the very end.
119.113: SAMECH. I hate the double-minded, but I love your law.
119.114: You are my hiding place and my shield; I hope in your word.
119.115: Depart from me, you evildoers, that I may keep the commandments of my God.
119.116: Uphold me according to your word that I may live and do not let me be ashamed of my hope.
119.117: Hold me up, that I may be safe and may look upon your statutes continually.
119.118: You have rejected all who stray from your statutes, for their deceit is falsehood.
119.119: All the wicked of the earth you remove like dross; therefore I love your testimonies.
119.120: My flesh trembles for fear of you, and I am in dread of your judgments.
119.121: AIN. I have done what is just and right; do not leave me to my oppressors.
119.122: Be a pledge of good to your servant; let not the proud oppress me.
119.123: My eyes fail from looking for your salvation and for the word of your righteousness.
119.124: Deal with your servant according to your steadfast love and teach me your statutes.
119.125: I am your servant; give me understanding that I may know your testimonies.
119.126: It is time for the LORD to act, for they have broken your law.
119.127: Therefore I love your commandments above gold, yes above fine gold.
119.128: Therefore I consider all your precepts about everything to be right and I hate every false way.
119.129: PE. Your testimonies are wonderful; therefore my soul keeps them.
119.130: The unfolding of your words gives light; it gives understanding to the simple.
119.131: I opened my mouth and panted, for I longed for your commandments.
119.132: Turn to me and be gracious to me, as you do to those who love your name.
119.133: Order my steps in your word and let no iniquity have dominion over me.
119.134: Deliver me from human oppression, and I will keep your precepts.
119.135: Make your face shine upon your servant and teach me your statutes.
119.136: Streams of water run down from my eyes because they do not keep your law.
119.137: TZADDI. Righteous are you, O LORD, and upright are your judgments.
119.138: You have commanded your testimonies in righteousness and in all faithfulness.
119.139: My zeal has consumed me because my enemies have forgotten your words.
119.140: Your word is very pure, and therefore your servant loves it.
119.141: I am small and despised, yet I do not forget your precepts.
119.142: Your righteousness is an everlasting righteousness, and your law is truth.
119.143: Trouble and anguish have found me out, yet your commandments are my delight.
119.144: Your testimonies are righteous forever; give me understanding that I may live.
119.145: KOPH. With my whole heart I cried; answer me, O LORD! I will keep your statutes.
119.146: I cried to you; save me, that I may keep your testimonies.
119.147: I rise before dawn and cry for help; I hope in your word.
119.148: My eyes are awake before the watches of the night, that I may meditate on your word.
119.149: Hear my voice according to your steadfast love, O LORD; revive me according to your justice.
119.150: They draw near who pursue evil purposes; they are far from your law.
119.151: But you are near, O LORD, and all your commandments are truth.
119.152: Long ago I learned from your testimonies that you have founded them forever.
119.153: RESH. Look upon my affliction and deliver me, for I do not forget your law.
119.154: Plead my cause and deliver me; give me life according to your word.
119.155: Salvation is far from the wicked, for they do not seek your statutes.
119.156: Great are your tender mercies, O LORD; revive me according to your judgments.
119.157: Many are my persecutors and my enemies, yet I have not turned aside from your testimonies.
119.158: I look at the faithless with loathing, because they do not keep your word.
119.159: Consider how I love your precepts! Revive me, O LORD, according to your steadfast love.
119.160: The sum of your word is truth, and every one of your righteous judgments endures forever.
119.161: SHIN. Princes have persecuted me without cause, but my heart stands in awe of your word.
119.162: I rejoice at your word like one who finds great spoil.
119.163: I hate and abhor lying, but I love your law.
119.164: Seven times a day I praise you for your righteous judgments.
119.165: Great peace have those who love your law; nothing causes them to stumble.
119.166: I hope for your salvation, O LORD, and I have done your commandments.
119.167: My soul has kept your testimonies and I love them exceedingly.
119.168: I have kept your precepts and your testimonies, for all my ways are before you.
119.169: TAV. Let my cry come before you, O LORD; give me understanding according to your word.
119.170: Let my plea come before you; deliver me according to your word.
119.171: My lips shall pour forth praise, for you teach me your statutes.
119.172: My tongue shall sing of your word, for all your commandments are righteous.
119.173: Let your hand be ready to help me, for I have chosen your precepts.
119.174: I long for your salvation, O LORD, and your law is my delight.
119.175: Let my soul live and it shall praise you; let your judgments help me.
119.176: I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek your servant, for I do not forget your commandments.

126.1: A Song of Ascents. When the LORD turned the captivity of Zion, we were like those who dream.
126.2: Then our mouth was filled with laughter and our tongue with shouts of joy; then they said among the nations, 'The LORD has done great things for them.'
126.3: The LORD has done great things for us; we are glad.
126.4: Restore our fortunes, O LORD, like streams in the Negev.
126.5: Those who sow in tears shall reap in joy.
126.6: He who goes forth weeping, bearing precious seed, shall come home with shouts of joy, bringing his sheaves with him.

127.1: A Song of Ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, those who build it labor in vain; unless the LORD keeps watch over the city, the watchman stays awake in vain.
127.2: It is in vain for you to rise early, to stay up late, to eat the bread of sorrows; for so he gives sleep to his beloved.
127.3: Behold, children are a heritage from the LORD; the fruit of the womb is his reward.
127.4: Like arrows in the hand of a warrior are the children of one's youth.
127.5: Blessed is the man who fills his quiver with them; he will not be put to shame when he speaks with his enemies in the gate.

128.1: A Song of Ascents. Blessed is everyone who fears the LORD, who walks in his ways.
128.2: The labor of your hands you shall eat; blessed shall you be, and it shall be well with you.
128.3: Your wife shall be like a fruitful vine in the innermost parts of your house; your children like olive shoots around your table.
128.4: Behold, thus shall the man be blessed who fears the LORD.
128.5: May the LORD bless you from Zion, and may you see the prosperity of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
128.6: May you see your children's children! Peace be upon Israel.

129.1: A Song of Ascents. 'Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth' — let Israel now say.
129.2: Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.
129.3: The plowers plowed upon my back; they made long their furrows.
129.4: The LORD is righteous; he has cut the cords of the wicked.
129.5: May all who hate Zion be put to shame and turned backward.
129.6: Let them be as the grass on the housetops, which withers before one can pluck it up.
129.7: With which the reaper does not fill his hand nor the binder of sheaves his arms,
129.8: nor do those who pass by say, 'The blessing of the LORD be upon you! We bless you in the name of the LORD.'

130.1: A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths I call to you, O LORD.
130.2: O Lord, hear my voice; let your ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
130.3: If you, O LORD, should mark iniquities, O Lord, who could stand?
130.4: But there is forgiveness with you, that you may be feared.
130.5: I wait for the LORD, my soul waits, and in his word I hope.
130.6: My soul longs for the Lord more than watchmen for the morning, more than watchmen for the morning.
130.7: Let Israel hope in the LORD, for with the LORD is steadfast love, and with him is plentiful redemption.
130.8: And he will redeem Israel from all his iniquities.

131.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. O LORD, my heart is not lifted up; my eyes are not raised too high; I do not occupy myself with great matters or with things too wonderful for me.
131.2: Surely I have calmed and quieted my soul, like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child is my soul within me.
131.3: Let Israel hope in the LORD from this time forth and forevermore.

132.1: A Song of Ascents. O LORD, remember David and all his afflictions,
132.2: how he swore to the LORD and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
132.3: 'I will not enter the tent of my house, nor go up into the bed I lie on;'
132.4: 'I will not give sleep to my eyes or slumber to my eyelids,'
132.5: 'until I find a place for the LORD, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.'
132.6: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah; we found it in the fields of Jaar.
132.7: Let us go to his dwelling place; let us worship at his footstool.
132.8: Arise, O LORD, to your resting place — you and the ark of your might.
132.9: Let your priests be clothed with righteousness, and let your faithful ones shout for joy.
132.10: For the sake of your servant David, do not turn away the face of your anointed one.
132.11: The LORD swore to David in truth — he will not turn back from it: 'Of the fruit of your body I will set one upon your throne.'
132.12: 'If your sons keep my covenant and my testimonies that I shall teach them, their sons also shall sit upon your throne forever.'
132.13: For the LORD has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his dwelling place.
132.14: 'This is my resting place forever; here I will dwell, for I have desired it.'
132.15: 'I will abundantly bless her provisions; I will satisfy her poor with bread.'
132.16: 'I will clothe her priests with salvation, and her faithful ones shall shout aloud for joy.'
132.17: 'There I will cause a horn to bud for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed.'
132.18: 'His enemies I will clothe with shame, but upon him his crown will shine.'

133.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant it is when brothers dwell together in unity!
133.2: It is like the precious oil upon the head, running down upon the beard, the beard of Aaron, running down to the collar of his garments!
133.3: It is like the dew of Hermon, like dew falling on the mountains of Zion, for there the LORD commanded the blessing — life forevermore.

134.1: A Song of Ascents. Come, bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD, who stand in the house of the LORD by night.
134.2: Lift up your hands toward the holy place and bless the LORD.
134.3: May the LORD, who made heaven and earth, bless you from Zion.

135.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD; praise him, O you servants of the LORD,
135.2: you who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God.
135.3: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; sing praises to his name, for it is pleasant.
135.4: For the LORD has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel as his treasured possession.
135.5: For I know that the LORD is great, and that our Lord is above all gods.
135.6: Whatever the LORD pleases, he does — in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all their depths.
135.7: He causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth; he makes lightning for the rain; he brings out the wind from his storehouses.
135.8: He struck down the firstborn of Egypt — both human and beast.
135.9: He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt — against Pharaoh and all his servants.
135.10: He struck down many nations and slew powerful kings —
135.11: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan —
135.12: and gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel his people.
135.13: Your name, O LORD, endures forever; your renown, O LORD, throughout all generations.
135.14: For the LORD will vindicate his people and will have compassion on his servants.
135.15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
135.16: They have mouths but cannot speak; they have eyes but cannot see;
135.17: they have ears but cannot hear; there is no breath at all in their mouths.
135.18: Those who make them become like them; so does everyone who trusts in them.
135.19: Bless the LORD, O house of Israel! Bless the LORD, O house of Aaron!
135.20: Bless the LORD, O house of Levi! You who fear the LORD, bless the LORD!
135.21: Blessed be the LORD from Zion, he who dwells in Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

136.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.2: Give thanks to the God of gods — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.3: Give thanks to the Lord of lords — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.4: To him who alone does great wonders — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.5: To him who made the heavens by wisdom — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.6: To him who spread out the earth upon the waters — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.7: To him who made the great lights — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.8: The sun to rule the day — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.9: The moon and stars to rule the night — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.10: To him who struck Egypt through their firstborn — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.11: And brought Israel out from among them — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.12: With a strong hand and an outstretched arm — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.13: To him who divided the Sea of Reeds in two — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.14: And made Israel pass through the midst of it — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.15: And swept Pharaoh and his army into the Sea of Reeds — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.16: To him who led his people through the wilderness — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.17: To him who struck down great kings — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.18: And killed renowned kings — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.19: Sihon, king of the Amorites — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.20: And Og, king of Bashan — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.21: And gave their land as an inheritance — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.22: An inheritance to Israel his servant — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.23: Who remembered us in our low estate — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.24: And rescued us from our enemies — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.25: Who gives food to all creatures — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.26: Give thanks to the God of heaven — for his steadfast love endures forever.

137.1: By the rivers of Babylon — there we sat down; yes, we wept when we remembered Zion.
137.2: We hung our harps on the willows in the midst of that land.
137.3: For there our captors demanded of us songs, and those who had devastated us demanded gladness, saying, 'Sing us one of the songs of Zion!'
137.4: How shall we sing the LORD's song in a foreign land?
137.5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand wither.
137.6: If I do not remember you, let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth, if I do not set Jerusalem above my highest joy.
137.7: Remember, O LORD, against the Edomites the day of Jerusalem — how they said, 'Tear it down, tear it down, right down to its foundations!'
137.8: O daughter of Babylon, doomed to be destroyed — blessed is he who repays you what you have done to us!
137.9: Blessed is he who seizes and dashes your infants against the rock.

138.1: A Psalm of David. I will praise you, O LORD, with my whole heart; before the gods I will sing your praise.
138.2: I will worship toward your holy temple and praise your name for your steadfast love and your faithfulness; for you have magnified your word above all your name.
138.3: On the day I called, you answered me; you made me bold and strong in my soul.
138.4: All the kings of the earth shall praise you, O LORD, when they hear the words of your mouth.
138.5: And they shall sing of the ways of the LORD, for great is the glory of the LORD.
138.6: For though the LORD is high, he regards the lowly; but the proud he knows from far away.
138.7: Though I walk in the midst of trouble, you revive me; you stretch out your hand against the wrath of my enemies, and your right hand saves me.
138.8: The LORD will perfect that which concerns me; your steadfast love, O LORD, endures forever — do not forsake the works of your hands.

139.1: To the chief Musician. A Psalm of David. O LORD, you have searched me and known me.
139.2: You know my sitting down and my rising up; you discern my thought from afar.
139.3: You search out my path and my lying down, and you are acquainted with all my ways.
139.4: For there is not a word on my tongue but, O LORD, you know it altogether.
139.5: You hem me in behind and before, and you have laid your hand upon me.
139.6: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain it.
139.7: Where shall I go from your Spirit? Or where shall I flee from your presence?
139.8: If I ascend to heaven, you are there; if I make my bed in Sheol, there you are.
139.9: If I take the wings of the morning and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea,
139.10: even there shall your hand lead me, and your right hand shall hold me fast.
139.11: If I say, 'Surely the darkness shall cover me, and the light around me become night' —
139.12: even the darkness is not dark to you; the night is as bright as the day, for darkness and light are alike to you.
139.13: For you formed my inward parts; you knit me together in my mother's womb.
139.14: I will praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made; marvellous are your works, and my soul knows it full well.
139.15: My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in secret, when I was woven in the depths of the earth.
139.16: Your eyes saw my unformed substance; in your book were written, all the days that were formed for me, when as yet there was none of them.
139.17: How precious to me are your thoughts, O God! How vast is the sum of them!
139.18: Were I to count them, they would outnumber the grains of sand; when I awake I am still with you.
139.19: O that you would slay the wicked, O God! Away from me, you men of blood!
139.20: who speak against you wickedly, and your enemies take your name in vain.
139.21: Do I not hate those who hate you, O LORD? And do I not loathe those who rise up against you?
139.22: I hate them with complete hatred; I count them my own enemies.
139.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart; test me and know my thoughts.
139.24: See if there is any grievous way in me, and lead me in the everlasting way.

140.1: To the chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Deliver me, O LORD, from evil men; preserve me from violent men,
140.2: who devise evil in their heart and stir up wars continually.
140.3: They have sharpened their tongues like a serpent; the poison of vipers is under their lips. Selah.
140.4: Keep me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked; preserve me from violent men who have purposed to trip my steps.
140.5: The proud have hidden a snare for me and cords; they have spread a net beside the path; they have set traps for me. Selah.
140.6: I say to the LORD, 'You are my God'; give ear, O LORD, to the voice of my supplications.
140.7: O Lord GOD, the strength of my salvation, you have covered my head in the day of battle.
140.8: Grant not, O LORD, the desires of the wicked; do not further their evil scheme, lest they be exalted. Selah.
140.9: As for the head of those who surround me, let the mischief of their own lips cover them.
140.10: Let burning coals fall upon them; let them be cast into the fire, into deep pits, never to rise again.
140.11: Let not a slanderer be established in the land; let evil hunt down the man of violence to ruin.
140.12: I know that the LORD will maintain the cause of the afflicted and the right of the poor.
140.13: Surely the righteous shall give thanks to your name; the upright shall dwell in your presence.

141.1: A Psalm of David. O LORD, I call upon you; hasten to me! Give ear to my voice when I cry to you.
141.2: Let my prayer be counted as incense before you, and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice.
141.3: Set a watch over my mouth, O LORD; keep guard over the door of my lips.
141.4: Do not let my heart incline to any evil thing, to practise deeds of wickedness with men who work iniquity, and let me not eat of their delicacies.
141.5: Let the righteous strike me — it is a kindness; let him reprove me — it is oil for my head; let my head not refuse it. Yet my prayer is still against their wicked deeds.
141.6: When their rulers are thrown down from rocky heights, they shall hear my words, for they are pleasant.
141.7: As when one plows and breaks up the earth, our bones are scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
141.8: But my eyes are toward you, O Lord GOD; in you I take refuge; do not leave my soul destitute.
141.9: Keep me from the trap they have laid for me, and from the snares of evildoers.
141.10: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, while I pass safely by.

142.1: A Maskil of David, a prayer when he was in the cave. With my voice I cry to the LORD; with my voice I make supplication to the LORD.
142.2: I pour out my complaint before him; I declare my trouble before him.
142.3: When my spirit is overwhelmed within me, you know my path. In the way where I walk they have hidden a snare for me.
142.4: I look to the right and see — there is no one who takes notice of me; refuge fails me; no one cares for my soul.
142.5: I cry to you, O LORD; I say, 'You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.'
142.6: Give heed to my cry, for I am brought very low; deliver me from my persecutors, for they are stronger than I.
142.7: Bring my soul out of prison, that I may give thanks to your name! The righteous will surround me, for you will deal bountifully with me.

143.1: A Psalm of David. Hear my prayer, O LORD; give ear to my supplications; in your faithfulness answer me, in your righteousness.
143.2: Enter not into judgment with your servant, for in your sight no living person is righteous.
143.3: For the enemy has pursued my soul; he has crushed my life to the ground; he has made me sit in darkness like those long dead.
143.4: Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed within me; my heart within me is appalled.
143.5: I remember the days of old; I meditate on all your deeds; I ponder the work of your hands.
143.6: I stretch out my hands to you; my soul thirsts for you like a parched land. Selah.
143.7: Answer me quickly, O LORD; my spirit fails! Hide not your face from me, lest I be like those who go down to the pit.
143.8: Let me hear of your steadfast love in the morning, for in you I trust; make me know the way I should walk, for to you I lift up my soul.
143.9: Deliver me from my enemies, O LORD; I flee to you for cover.
143.10: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God; let your good Spirit lead me on level ground.
143.11: For your name's sake, O LORD, revive me! In your righteousness bring my soul out of trouble.
143.12: And in your steadfast love cut off my enemies, and destroy all who afflict my soul, for I am your servant.

150.1: Praise the LORD! Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in the firmament of his power.
150.2: Praise him for his mighty acts; praise him according to his abundant greatness.
150.3: Praise him with the blast of the shofar; praise him with lyre and harp.
150.4: Praise him with tambourine and dance; praise him with strings and pipe.
150.5: Praise him with resounding cymbals; praise him with clashing cymbals.
150.6: Let every breathing thing praise the LORD! Praise the LORD!

144.1: Blessed be the LORD, my rock, who trains my hands for war and my fingers for battle.
144.2: My steadfast love and my fortress, my high tower and my deliverer, my shield and he in whom I take refuge, who subdues my people under me.
144.3: O LORD, what is man that you take knowledge of him, or the son of man that you give thought to him?
144.4: Man is like a breath; his days are like a passing shadow.
144.5: O LORD, bow your heavens and come down; touch the mountains and they shall smoke.
144.6: Flash forth lightning and scatter them; shoot out your arrows and rout them.
144.7: Stretch out your hand from on high; rescue me and deliver me from great waters, from the hand of foreigners,
144.8: whose mouth speaks vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
144.9: A new song will I sing to you, O God; upon a psaltery of ten strings will I sing praises to you,
144.10: who gives salvation to kings, who delivers David his servant from the deadly sword.
144.11: Rescue me and deliver me from the hand of foreigners, whose mouth speaks vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
144.12: That our sons may be as plants grown up in their youth; that our daughters may be as corner stones, polished after the similitude of a palace;
144.13: That our garners may be full, affording all manner of store; that our sheep may bring forth thousands and ten thousands in our fields;
144.14: That our oxen may be strong to labour; that there be no breaking in, no going out, and no crying in our streets;
144.15: Blessed is that people that is in such a case; blessed is that people whose God is the LORD.

145.1: I will extol you, my God and King, and bless your name forever and ever.
145.2: Every day I will bless you; and I will praise your name forever and ever.
145.3: Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; and his greatness is unsearchable.
145.4: One generation shall praise your works to another, and shall declare your mighty acts.
145.5: I will speak of the glorious honour of your majesty, and of your wondrous works.
145.6: And men shall speak of the might of your terrible acts; and I will declare your greatness.
145.7: They shall abundantly utter the memory of your great goodness, and shall sing of your righteousness.
145.8: The LORD is gracious and merciful; slow to anger, and of great steadfast love.
145.9: The LORD is good to all; and his tender mercies are over all his works.
145.10: All your works shall praise you, O LORD; and your saints shall bless you.
145.11: They shall speak of the glory of your kingdom, and talk of your power;
145.12: To make known to the sons of man your mighty acts, and the glorious majesty of your kingdom.
145.13: Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and your dominion endures throughout all generations.
145.14: The LORD upholds all who fall, and raises up all who are bowed down.
145.15: The eyes of all wait upon you; and you give them their food in due season.
145.16: You open your hand, and satisfy the desire of every living thing.
145.17: The LORD is righteous in all his ways, and gracious in all his works.
145.18: The LORD is near to all who call upon him, to all who call upon him in truth.
145.19: He will fulfil the desire of those who fear him; he will hear their cry and save them.
145.20: The LORD preserves all who love him, but all the wicked will he destroy.
145.21: My mouth shall speak the praise of the LORD; and let all flesh bless his holy name forever and ever.

146.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD, O my soul!
146.2: I will praise the LORD while I live; I will sing praises to my God while I have being.
146.3: Put not your trust in princes, nor in the son of man, in whom there is no help.
146.4: His breath departs; he returns to the earth; in that very day his thoughts perish.
146.5: Blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD his God,
146.6: who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them; who keeps truth forever;
146.7: who executes judgment for the oppressed; who gives food to the hungry. The LORD looses the prisoners;
146.8: the LORD opens the eyes of the blind; the LORD raises up those who are bowed down; the LORD loves the righteous.
146.9: The LORD preserves the strangers; he relieves the fatherless and widow; but the way of the wicked he turns upside down.
146.10: The LORD shall reign forever, your God, O Zion, unto all generations. Praise the LORD.

147.1: Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God; for it is pleasant, and praise is comely.
147.2: The LORD builds up Jerusalem; he gathers together the outcasts of Israel.
147.3: He heals the brokenhearted, and binds up their wounds.
147.4: He counts the number of the stars; he calls them all by their names.
147.5: Great is our Lord, and of great power; his understanding is infinite.
147.6: The LORD lifts up the meek; he casts the wicked down to the ground.
147.7: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; sing praises upon the harp to our God;
147.8: who covers the heaven with clouds, who prepares rain for the earth, who makes grass to grow upon the mountains.
147.9: He gives to the beast its food, and to the young ravens which cry.
147.10: He does not delight in the strength of the horse; he does not take pleasure in the legs of a man.
147.11: The LORD takes pleasure in those who fear him, in those who hope in his steadfast love.
147.12: Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem; praise your God, O Zion.
147.13: For he has strengthened the bars of your gates; he has blessed your children within you.
147.14: He makes peace in your borders, and fills you with the finest of the wheat.
147.15: He sends forth his commandment upon the earth; his word runs very swiftly.
147.16: He gives snow like wool; he scatters the hoarfrost like ashes.
147.17: He casts forth his ice like morsels; who can stand before his cold?
147.18: He sends out his word, and melts them; he causes his wind to blow, and the waters flow.
147.19: He declares his word to Jacob, his statutes and his judgments to Israel.
147.20: He has not dealt so with any nation; and as for his judgments, they have not known them. Praise the LORD.

148.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD from the heavens; praise him in the heights!
148.2: Praise him, all his angels; praise him, all his hosts!
148.3: Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all you stars of light!
148.4: Praise him, you heavens of heavens, and you waters that are above the heavens.
148.5: Let them praise the name of the LORD; for he commanded, and they were created.
148.6: He has also established them forever and ever; he has made a decree which shall not pass away.
148.7: Praise the LORD from the earth, you dragons, and all deeps;
148.8: fire and hail; snow and vapour; stormy wind fulfilling his word;
148.9: mountains and all hills; fruitful trees and all cedars;
148.10: beasts and all cattle; creeping things and flying fowl;
148.11: kings of the earth and all peoples; princes and all judges of the earth;
148.12: both young men and maidens; old men and children:
148.13: let them praise the name of the LORD; for his name alone is excellent; his glory is above the earth and heaven.
148.14: He also exalts the horn of his people, the praise of all his saints; even of the children of Israel, a people near to him. Praise the LORD.

149.1: Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song, and his praise in the congregation of the saints.
149.2: Let Israel rejoice in him that made him; let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.
149.3: Let them praise his name in the dance; let them sing praises to him with the timbrel and harp.
149.4: For the LORD takes pleasure in his people; he will beautify the meek with salvation.
149.5: Let the saints be joyful in glory; let them sing aloud upon their beds.
149.6: Let the high praises of God be in their mouth, and a two-edged sword in their hand;
149.7: to execute vengeance upon the heathen, and punishments upon the peoples;
149.8: to bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron;
149.9: to execute upon them the judgment written; this honour have all his saints. Praise the LORD.
